participants.name: 한양대학교 (jduobt)
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교 전자공학부 조교수로서 미래인재양성사업 교육연구단의 연구교수로 활동 중입니다. 3년 전 미국에서 박사 후 연구를 마치고 귀국했습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 반도체 소자 및 집적회로 설계, 연구 성과 분석 및 컨설팅 보고서 작성 경험.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구단의 국제화 역량을 높이고, 참여 대학원생들의 우수 논문 게재를 지원하여 연구단 평가 지표를 개선하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 부산에서
participants.professional_persona: 부산에서 태어나 자란 전기전자공학 박사과정생으로, 무선통신 시스템 최적화 연구에 매진하고 있습니다. BK21 혁신인재양성사업의 지원을 받아 월 130만 원의 장학금으로 연구에 집중할 수 있게 되었습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: MATLAB과 C++ 기반 시뮬레이션에 강점이 있으며, 딥러닝을 통한 채널 추정 알고리즘 개발 경험이 있습니다. 국제 학술지에 2편의 논문을 게재했습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 통신 분야 글로벌 기업이나 국책연구소에서 연구원으로 근무하며 6G 핵심 기술 개발에 기여하는 것이 꿈입니다. 향후 Post-Doc을 통해 연구 역량을 더 쌓고 싶습니다.

participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-j6xuhc43

participants.skills_and_expertise: 전기화학 분석, 배터리 소재 합성 및 특성 평가에 전문성을 갖추었으며, 실험 데이터 분석에 파이썬을 활용할 줄 압니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 졸업 후 국책 연구소에 취업하여 차세대 이차전지 기술 개발에 기여하고, 장기적으로는 에너지 분야 전문가로 성장하고자 합니다.

participants.name: 서울대학교
participants.professional_persona: 서울대학교 사회학과에서 박사학위를 받고 현재 동 대학 BK21 교육연구단에서 신진연구자로 활동 중입니다. 디지털 전환과 사회적 불평등에 관한 연구를 수행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 대규모 설문조사 설계 및 통계 분석(SPSS, Stata)에 능숙하며, 질적 연구 방법론과 혼합연구법에도 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: BK21 지원을 통해 데이터 기반 사회정책 연구를 심화하여, 디지털 격차 해소에 기여하는 연구자로 성장하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 전남대학교
participants.professional_persona: 전남대학교 기계공학과에서 박사학위를 받고 현재 광주과학기술원 BK21 교육연구단에서 박사후연구원으로 근무합니다. 지능형 로봇과 자율주행 시스템을 연구하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: ROS 기반 로봇 소프트웨어 개발, 센서 퓨전, 딥러닝 기반 영상인식 기술을 보유하고 있으며, 실험실에서 실제 로봇 플랫폼을 운용한 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 자율주행과 서비스 로봇 분야의 실용 기술을 고도화하여, 지역 산업체와 협력해 현장에 바로 적용할 수 있는 솔루션을 만들고 싶습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher at Hanyang University's BK21 program, I estimate the total funding allocated to Hanyang for 2022 based on the national BK21 budget of 408,080 million KRW, which is distributed across 68 universities and 577 education research groups. Hanyang, being a major research university, likely receives a proportional share—roughly 5-6% of the total education research group budget (355,151 million KRW), plus a portion of the graduate innovation support fund (52,896 million KRW). For graduate scholarship slots, the monthly stipends (master's 700K, doctoral 1.3M, doctoral candidate 1M KRW) imply an average of about 1M KRW per slot per month, so with Hanyang's share of the budget, I estimate around 19,000 slots annually across all groups at Hanyang, though this is a rough aggregate. Postdoc positions are fewer because the minimum salary of 3M KRW per month is higher; based on typical group sizes and budget constraints, I estimate about 1,200 postdoc positions at Hanyang. These numbers reflect my perspective as a researcher who sees the program's capacity to support many graduate students but fewer postdocs due to higher per-person costs.
evidence: The national BK21 budget for 2022 is 408,080 million KRW, and Hanyang University, as a large institution with multiple education research groups, likely receives a significant share—roughly 5-6% of the total education research group budget of 355,151 million KRW, plus graduate innovation funds.

Graduate stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW/month) average about 1M KRW per slot per month, so with Hanyang's estimated budget of around 20 billion KRW for graduate support, approximately 19,000 slots can be funded annually (20B / 12 months / 1M ≈ 1,667 slots per month, but annual slots count each student-month, so 19,000 reflects total monthly slots across the year).

Postdoc minimum salary of 3M KRW per month is three times higher than the average graduate stipend, so postdoc positions are fewer; based on typical group budgets and the 10% operating cost limit, I estimate about 1,200 postdoc positions at Hanyang.

Eligibility criteria (field classification, enrollment, degree level) limit the number of education research groups at Hanyang to around 20-30 groups, each supporting a mix of graduate students and postdocs, which aligns with my estimates.

initial_posts.narrative: As a research professor at Hanyang University's electronics engineering education research group, I estimate the total funding allocated to Hanyang for the BK21 program in 2022 based on the national budget of 408,080 million KRW distributed across 577 groups. Hanyang, as a major research university, likely hosts around 15-20 education research groups, each receiving an average of 600-800 million KRW. Given our group's focus on semiconductor devices, we typically receive about 1.2 billion KRW annually, which aligns with the upper end for science and engineering fields. For graduate scholarship slots, with monthly stipends of 700K-1.3M KRW, the average cost per slot per year is about 12 million KRW (1M/month * 12 months). Our group's budget for scholarships is roughly 2.16 billion KRW (1.2B * 0.6 for personnel costs, with 70% for students), yielding about 180 slots. Postdoc positions are fewer due to the higher minimum salary of 3M KRW per month (36M/year). With about 540 million KRW allocated for postdocs (1.2B * 0.3 for personnel, then 50% for postdocs), we can support around 15 postdocs. This is consistent with our group's current composition. I disagree with the Seoul National University researcher's estimate of 19,000 slots and 1,200 postdocs for Hanyang, as those numbers seem inflated for a single university—they likely reflect national totals or a misunderstanding of Hanyang's share. Hanyang has about 15-20 groups, not 577, so our slots are proportionally smaller.
evidence: Hanyang University hosts approximately 15-20 education research groups, and our electronics engineering group typically receives around 1.2 billion KRW annually, which is consistent with the upper range for science and engineering fields in the BK21 program.

Graduate scholarship slots are calculated based on an average monthly stipend of 1 million KRW per student, leading to an annual cost of 12 million KRW per slot; with 60% of our budget allocated to personnel and 70% of that to students, we can support about 180 slots.

Postdoc positions require a minimum salary of 3 million KRW per month (36 million KRW per year); with 30% of our personnel budget for postdocs, we can fund approximately 15 positions, matching our current team size.

The Seoul National University researcher's estimate of 19,000 slots and 1,200 postdocs for Hanyang appears to be a national aggregate rather than Hanyang-specific, as Hanyang's share of the 577 groups is only about 3%, not 100%.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher at Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology, I estimate that Hanyang University's BK21 program will disburse approximately 12 billion KRW in total funds, following the 70% March / 30% September schedule. This is based on the prior phase's consensus that Hanyang hosts about 15-20 education research groups, each receiving around 600-800 million KRW on average. The funds are transferred efficiently as per the policy's two-installment plan, with the first payment in March covering the bulk of operational needs. Evaluation panels are convened for annual, mid-term, and compliance purposes—typically three formal meetings per year for each group, but since Hanyang has multiple groups, the total number of panel meetings across all groups is about 3 (one annual, one mid-term, one compliance) for the entire university's BK21 program, as these are centralized evaluations. Consulting sessions are offered to groups that request them; based on the policy context, 280 out of 578 groups nationally requested consulting, so proportionally for Hanyang's 15-20 groups, I estimate about 280 sessions total across all groups (since consulting is provided per group request, and Hanyang's groups likely participate similarly). Compliance checks are executed on 205 groups nationally for innovative talent training; Hanyang's share of these checks is proportional, so I estimate 205 checks for the entire program, but for Hanyang specifically, given its 15-20 groups, the number is about 205 (since the policy states 205 groups are subject to compliance checks, and Hanyang's groups are part of that national total). I disagree with any estimate that would suggest lower consulting or compliance activity, as the policy clearly mandates these activities for all groups.
evidence: The policy states that funds are disbursed in two installments: 70% in March and 30% in September, and Hanyang's total funding of 12 billion KRW is consistent with the prior phase's estimate of 15-20 groups each receiving 600-800 million KRW.

Evaluation panels are convened for annual, mid-term, and compliance evaluations, and for Hanyang's centralized BK21 program, three formal panel meetings are typical per year, covering all groups collectively.

Consulting sessions are provided to groups that request them; nationally, 280 out of 578 groups requested consulting, so Hanyang's 15-20 groups likely participate proportionally, leading to about 280 sessions across all groups.
Compliance checks are executed on 205 groups nationally for innovative talent training, and Hanyang's groups are part of this national count, so the number of checks for Hanyang is approximately 205.

These estimates are grounded in my personal experience in a productive engineering group and the typical output rates I observe among my peers.

In my experience, each graduate student typically produces 2-3 publications per year (including journal articles and conference papers), and postdocs produce about 4-5. Given that not all students are equally productive, I estimate an average of 3 publications per supported participant (graduate + postdoc) per year, yielding about 540 publications total. For international conference presentations, about half of the participants attend at least one international conference per year, so I estimate 90 presentations (50% of 195 participants). These estimates are grounded in my personal experience in a productive engineering group and the typical output rates I observe among my peers. I note that other participants have similar estimates, but I maintain my own reasoning based on my group's actual productivity.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student in electrical engineering at Hanyang University, I see the BK21 program outcomes from my group's perspective. Based on the prior phases, our group receives about 1.2 billion KRW annually, supporting roughly 180 graduate slots and 15 postdoc positions. The outputs phase showed 540 publications and 90 international conference presentations from our group. For outcomes, I estimate the graduate employment rate within one year of graduation at 88.9%, based on my observation that most of my peers secure jobs in industry or research institutes within a year, though some take longer due to postdoc or job search. The international collaboration rate is about 50%, as half of our participants co-author papers with foreign researchers or attend international conferences, supported by program incentives like conference travel funds. The full-time faculty lecture ratio is around 75%, as most graduate courses are taught by tenure-track professors, though some specialized or language courses are taught by part-time lecturers. The evaluation and consulting feedback loops help improve these rates by identifying weaknesses and adjusting training. I disagree with any higher estimates for employment rate because the job market is competitive, and not all graduates find jobs immediately.

Evaluation and consulting feedback loops, such as annual self-evaluations and consulting sessions, help identify weaknesses in training and collaboration, leading to targeted improvements that boost employment and collaboration rates.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher at GIST, I see sustained BK21 investment over a decade gradually improving South Korea's S&T talent competitiveness. The program's focus on graduate training, international collaboration, and research integrity will likely move Korea from its current rank around 10-12 to about 8th globally, as alumni become leaders in academia and industry. The high-impact publication rate per 1000 researchers will increase modestly to about 12.5, driven by better mentoring, interdisciplinary collaboration, and dissemination of best practices from top groups like Hanyang's. However, systemic changes in research culture take time, and the impact within the 2021-2022 window is still early-stage.
evidence: Sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade builds a critical mass of highly skilled researchers who drive innovation, improving national competitiveness rankings gradually.
As BK21 alumni progress to faculty and industry R&D roles, they promote research integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity, enhancing the quality and impact of research outputs.

Dissemination of outstanding group achievements and operational know-how through publications and forums raises the baseline performance of other institutions, boosting overall S&T ecosystem productivity.
Within the 2021-2022 window, the program is still in early stages, so improvements in competitiveness and high-impact publications are modest but measurable, reflecting initial cohort effects.
